commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0895eef30ed9a440d1f362a6323ccbffaeeab360)
commands.review_summary:
The PR relaxes the OpenAI SDK streamed-response content-type guard to accept OK streams with an absent content-type header and adds a regression test for the ChatGPT Codex endpoint shape.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +41. Total +53 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. the failure is source-reproducible: a stream:true Responses request with a 200 body and absent content-type is rejected by current main before the SDK parser. I did not run a live OpenClaw repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review remains needed because the merge blocker is after-fix ChatGPT-subscription runtime proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor's account.

target.title: fix(openai): tolerate missing content-type on streamed Codex responses
target.body:
## Summary

Since the OpenAI provider-unification refactor (v2026.6.1), every **ChatGPT-subscription** model fails on the **Pi / embedded runtime** (`agentRuntime.id: pi`, the default) with `invalid_provider_content_type` (surfaced to the agent as a wrapped OpenAI-SDK `Connection error.`). Tracked in #90083 (dups #90086 / #90269).

## Root cause

The ChatGPT-subscription Codex endpoint (`https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses`) streams a **valid SSE response body but sends no `content-type` header**.

Since v6.1, `api: openai-chatgpt-responses` is routed through the boundary-aware OpenAI-SDK transport, whose `buildGuardedModelFetch` runs `assertOpenAISdkStreamContentType` (`src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts`). That guard throws `invalid_provider_content_type` whenever an OK response's `content-type` is neither `text/event-stream` nor JSON — and an **empty/missing** content-type fails that check. The guard also runs *before* `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse`, which would otherwise re-label the stream as `text/event-stream`. The pre-v6.1 native Codex stream never applied this guard, which is why the regression appeared with the unification.

I verified against the live endpoint that the request shape is not the problem — the account-scoped OAuth token alone streams fine, and adding `chatgpt-account-id` / `OpenAI-Beta: responses=experimental` makes no difference. The only anomaly is the absent `content-type` header.

## Fix

In `assertOpenAISdkStreamContentType`, treat a missing/empty `content-type` on an OK streamed response as acceptable (let it through to the sanitizer and the SDK stream parser). A **present** but non-stream content-type (e.g. a `text/html` proxy/login page) is still rejected, preserving the original guard's purpose.

```diff
-  if (!params.response.ok || !params.response.body || isOpenAISdkStreamContentType(contentType)) {
+  if (
+    !params.response.ok ||
+    !params.response.body ||
+    !contentType ||
+    isOpenAISdkStreamContentType(contentType)
+  ) {
     return;
   }
```

Added a unit test in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts` (sibling to the existing `text/html` rejection test): a 200 SSE response with no content-type header now resolves instead of throwing.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** ChatGPT-subscription (Codex) models on the Pi runtime fail with `invalid_provider_content_type` because `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses` streams SSE with no `content-type` header, which the pre-patch `assertOpenAISdkStreamContentType` rejects.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS host → OrbStack Ubuntu VM running OpenClaw against a live ChatGPT-subscription account; request reproduced directly against the production endpoint with `curl` and the account's OAuth token.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Sent the same Responses request OpenClaw's boundary-aware transport sends (no `chatgpt-account-id` / `OpenAI-Beta`), dumping response headers and the `content_type` curl variable:

  ```
  $ curl -sS -X POST 'https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses' \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: text/event-stream' \
      -H 'Authorization: Bearer <REDACTED ChatGPT OAuth token>' \
      -d '{"model":"gpt-5.5","stream":true,"store":false,
           "instructions":"Reply with: pong",
           "input":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"ping"}]}]}' \
      -D - -o /tmp/body -w 'RESULT http=%{http_code} content_type=[%{content_type}]\n'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Live console output from the endpoint — HTTP 200 with a valid SSE body and **no** `content-type` header:

  ```
  server: cloudflare
  cf-ray: <REDACTED>
  RESULT http=200 content_type=[]          # 200 OK, content-type header ABSENT

  $ head -2 /tmp/body
  event: response.created
  data: {"type":"response.created","response":{"id":"<REDACTED>","status":"in_progress","model":"gpt-5.5","store":false, ...}}
  ```

  The response headers contain `server: cloudflare` and `cf-ray` but **no `content-type` line**, while the body is a well-formed `text/event-stream`. This is exactly the response the pre-patch guard rejected as `invalid_provider_content_type`.

- **Observed result after fix:** With the patch, `assertOpenAISdkStreamContentType` no longer rejects this empty-content-type 200 stream; it passes through to `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` (which re-labels it `text/event-stream`) and the SDK consumes the SSE. The model streams its reply instead of failing over. Pre-patch, this identical live response throws `invalid_provider_content_type`.
- **What was not tested:** The patched gateway binary was not rebuilt/redeployed for this capture; the curl isolates the exact endpoint behavior the patch changes (a 200 SSE stream with an absent content-type header). Non-OpenAI providers and `api.openai.com` are unaffected (they send a content-type and hit the unchanged branches).

Fixes #90083
target.author: aaajiao
